currentFocus: Audit the 29-row patch/profile contract, harden dependency and copied-target invariants, then close one bounded evidence-backed patch/mod or launch-attestation gap.

product.patchCatalog: 29 total rows; 20 visible options (9 stable, 11 experimental); 9 hidden companions
product.safeCopyPosture: retain: installed game and original BEA.exe remain read-only; mutations use copied targets and app-owned roots

winUiFirstUseCloseout.status: accepted in the current source slice
behavior: True first run distinguishes unset, invalid, and ready game-folder states and prioritizes setup while preserving manual Save Lab file browsing.
A temporarily unavailable remembered game folder remains visible as Needs review and survives unrelated preference saves instead of being erased.
Shell navigation has one owner, page-scoped status filtering, generation-guarded arrival focus, active Media-tab focus, and one semantic Level 1 heading per principal page.
Launch preset selection is derived from effective controls; debug trace, normalized text, Admin Level, manual level edits, and the local split-screen helper stay synchronized.
Both copied-executable source action groups stack and wrap at compact width.
Audio-first and restored-Video page construction defer native VLC; current-session Video demand and real local playback remain functional.
Visual fixture catalogs use schema 2 and capture evidence rejects incompletely rendered headers.

12 explicit Home state/route UIA cases passed
shell accessibility and restored-Video lazy-VLC UIA passed
compact Patch Bench interaction UIA passed
Markdown links, notices, public allowlist/hard-payload, and repo hygiene passed

filesystemSafety.summary: Save/options, asset catalog/package, downstream package, Python generated output, and C# AYA output paths use bounded roots, held identities, guarded writes, hostile-path tests, and final byte/identity verification.
filesystemSafety.residualBoundary: After finalized bytes leave delete-pending quarantine, a same-user process can copy them or create a byte-identical hardlink; no confidentiality after finalization is claimed.

rebuildCore.architecture: GPL-3.0-or-later RE-informed original-code net8 Core plus headless command-tape verifier; Core owns deterministic simulation and has no filesystem, process, clock, network, presentation, package, project-reference, or custom-build inputs
rebuildCore.implemented: fixed-step integer simulation, walker/jet transformation, bounded movement, energy/shield state, held-fire cooldowns, projectiles/targets, reset, immutable snapshots, strict frozen command tapes, canonical continuation-state and rolling input/post-step trace hashing, independent built-in goldens, bounded replay work, and distinct headless exit semantics
rebuildCore.acceptance: Fresh source gates passed: zero-warning npm test with 1307 AppCore, 136 WinUI plus 2 expected private-catalog skips, and 38 rebuild tests; 100 repeated first-flight runs matched trace 235f4e28a9c7cce0da79bafdd29929f9a2beceb818005abd24ee7802e9c7f4d1 and final state 0fd93451622e272e9f413472df3f9b0505d7a618f077194b27348305f8aa9d01; historical-register, docsync, command-doc, 3662-file/6264-link Markdown, 19419-file hard-payload, 19603-file submodule allowlist, migration, hygiene, 74-package notices, release-profile, and 687-file curated-manifest gates passed. The fixture-free candidate materialized exactly 692 files; pre-build inventory/payload and 463-file/138-link checks passed; standalone npm test passed a zero-warning WinUI build, 1307 AppCore tests, 123 WinUI tests with 13 explicit real-save-dependent plus 2 private-catalog skips, and 38 rebuild tests; post-build notices/hygiene passed. A focused producer/consumer contract also restored, rebuilt, and passed 1/1 from a non-sibling temporary root.
rebuildCore.review: Codex normal and adversarial Core follow-ups accepted after their blockers were fixed; focused Codex adversarial export review accepted payload-mode command construction. Cursor/Grok normal accepted the revised self-contained package architecture. Cursor/Grok adversarial first blocked the unattested real-save export and ambient-input/exclusion evidence, then accepted after the save returned to R4_DENY, negative-surface tests passed, the fixture-free artifact passed, and the producer contract passed from a non-sibling root. Codex root retains final acceptance.

rebuildFirstFlight.architecture: Godot 4.7 is a procedural view/input adapter over the pure net8 Client and deterministic Core; OnslaughtRebuild.slnx stays engine-free and the visual solution is separate
rebuildFirstFlight.input: exact rational 30 Hz accumulation, 250 ms frame cap, held input sampling, one-shot toggle/reset edges, one-tick movement/fire pulses, focus-handler clear, and neutral rearm
rebuildFirstFlight.toolchain: fixed tracked manifest and SHA-256, exact official archive/runtime identity, 82-file tree, fixed per-user cache, reparse rejection, held read-only file lease, setup lock through consumers, locked archive-local Godot restore, and explicit same-user non-sandbox boundary
rebuildFirstFlight.acceptance: Source npm test passed a zero-warning WinUI build, 1307 AppCore, 136 WinUI plus 2 expected private-catalog skips, 38 Core, 21 Client, 12 toolchain, 6 evidence-validator, and 6 owned-process tests. Cache-only native smoke passed at 1280x720 and 1200x675 with tick 120, state hash 87b9d09a3afec0bebd97f06793b83baabe3ff330b2378ff149194d6d6a4f779d, heuristic semantic anchors, synthetic notification-handler clear/rearm, and kernel-owned Job Object cleanup. The exact launcher was manually played with Q/W/Space/R/Esc and exited without an orphan. Full docs, 19453-file payload, 19637-file submodule payload, 18600-file hygiene, 74-package notices, and curated gates passed.
rebuildFirstFlight.package: The 721-selected/726-materialized exact source candidate passed payload/inventory, 463-file/138-link Markdown, standalone zero-warning npm test with 1307 AppCore, 123 WinUI plus 15 expected fixture/catalog skips, all 83 rebuild tests, 74-package notices, and 718-file hygiene. First Flight remains source-only and is not in the published v1.0.9 WinUI ZIP.
rebuildFirstFlight.review: Codex and bounded Cursor/Grok normal/adversarial reviews accepted the slice after input, toolchain, smoke-evidence, package, and process-ownership challenges were fixed. The final Codex adversarial follow-up accepted the suspended-root kill-on-close Job Object and explicitly heuristic visual-evidence boundary.

boundaries: No installed-game or original BEA.exe mutation.
No release, signing, installer/MSIX/Store, Host/Join, online-ready, gameplay-parity, visual-parity, or rebuild-parity claim.
Current rebuild implementation must be labeled RE-informed unless a separately staffed sealed-spec process and license review support a stronger clean-room claim.

activeBlockers: Native AYA runtime compatibility smoke is unavailable because the reference extractor requires Visual C++ targets not installed on this machine; synthetic harness output passed.
